In Jan 2021, I started self-learning Kubernetes to re-skill and cloud ready engg. I'm currently working on public cloud related project. I would like to continue Cloud Native tech journey with you, thanks!

person.name: Manuel Vogel
person.bio: Hi, I'm Manuel, a Freelance AWS Solution Architect & DevOps engineer by heart with a broad set of skills strong collaboration and knowledge-sharing abilities. My aim is to minimize your risks by setting standards on software architecture and quality, deployment strategies, pipelines, and automation. I run the AWS User Group Frankfurt as a co-leader.

person.name: Aaron Zhilong Wang
person.bio: Zhilong Wang is a Principal Engineer specializing in cloud-native and distributed systems, with expertise in Serverless, Service Mesh, and cloud platforms. He has 10+ years of core architecture and practical engineering experience at Tencent, Alibaba, and JD.com, continuously improving the performance and reliability of cloud-native systems under data-intensive and compute-intensive workloads, including complex environments involving GPU/CPU heterogeneous resource coordination and mixed online–offline deployments. He also actively contributes to CNCF open-source projects such as Kubernetes, MOSN, and Layotto.

person.name: Manuel Wegel
person.bio: Senior Platform Engineer with 6+ years of hands-on Kubernetes experience, specializing in cloud-native infrastructure, GitOps workflows with ArgoCD, and Kubernetes operator development using the Kopf framework. I work primarily in healthcare and public sector environments, building production-grade platforms on Rancher and OpenShift. My CNCF journey started with CKA and grew into the full Kubestronaut path – CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCNA, and KCSA – driven by a passion for deep Kubernetes expertise and the cloud-native ecosystem. Currently pursuing the Golden Kubestronaut path with ICA, CAPA, and CGOA as next targets.

person.name: Khalil Chalbi
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Cloud consultant at Exalt in France, where I work as a Senior Kubernetes Engineer. I am passionate about virtualization and cloud-native technologies, and I design, operate, and secure production Kubernetes clusters. I also mentor junior engineers, helping them build strong skills in Kubernetes and the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Petr Černohouz
person.bio: DevOps Engineer specializing in Kubernetes cluster management and high-performance CI/CD pipeline design. Experienced in optimizing software delivery across cloud and on-prem environments. Passionate about automating workflows and sharing knowledge through Docker and Kubernetes training.

person.name: Sebastian Meindorfer
person.bio: I am a Cloud Platform Architect specializing in AWS and CNCF technologies, with expertise in Kubernetes, EKS and OpenShift, GitOps with ArgoCD, and networking with Cilium. My certifications in platform engineering, security, and automation also strengthen my freelance projects on the side, where I apply cloud-native best practices. I stay engaged with the community by attending Kubernetes and cloud-native Meetups and exploring emerging trends such as AI-Ops.

person.name: Shunsuke Yoshikawa
person.bio: I have several years of experience as an engineer in the Cloud Native domain, primarily focusing on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). My expertise lies in the design, construction, and operation of cloud infrastructure. I am also a member of the organizing team for the Platform Engineering Meetup community in Japan.

person.name: Arush Sharma
person.bio: I'm Arush Sharma, a maintainer and contributor to AWS Controllers for Kubernetes (ACK) and Kube Resource Orchestrator (KRO). At AWS, I work on the Amazon EKS team, where I focus on simplifying Kubernetes-native APIs for managing AWS services. Recently, I've been enhancing GitOps workflows by leveraging KRO's ResourceGraphDefinitions (RGDs) to create reusable, declarative templates that streamline the deployment of AWS resources at scale.

person.name: Chung Ng
person.bio: A passionate DevOps engineer, dedicated to optimizing software delivery through open-source technologies and platform engineering. Enjoyed collaborating with teams to enhance deployment processes and drive innovation. Enthused in cloud native software architecture design

person.name: Karena Angell
person.bio: Karena Angell is a member of the CNCF Technical Oversight Committee (TOC) and was a Technical Lead for the CNCF Technical Advisory Group (TAG) App Delivery. She is also a Senior Principal Technical Product Manager at Red Hat focused on upstream projects, including Kubernetes. She has a broad background within various disciplines including Product Management, Enterprise Architecture, Enterprise Solution design and management, Marketing, IT Service Management and technical community building. She has also worked extensively within Commercial and Government enterprises. These experiences give her a unique understanding of how to guide strategic efforts to engage community members and drive increased CNCF involvement.

person.name: Michel Van Rijn
person.bio: All-round IT engineer, passionate about learning. Archlinux and Neovim enthusiast

person.name: Cahit Yusuf Kafadar
person.bio: As a passionate consultant specializing in cloud-native technologies, I have a wealth of experience in designing and optimizing modern cloud solutions. My work focuses on empowering organizations to enhance their agility, scalability, and operational efficiency through cutting-edge cloud-native practices. With a strong dedication to innovation, I strive to deliver tailored solutions that align with the unique goals of every business I collaborate with.

person.name: Joël Schneider
person.bio: Master Graduate in Computer Science. Been in the security field for four years. Working on cloud native operators. Fascinated by the  breadth of technologies in the cloud native universe.

person.name: Meïssa Birima Couly Mbaye
person.bio: I'm Currently a FullStack Software & DevOps Engineer, based in Dakar, Senegal and Technical Team Lead. Apart from the code, I am passionate about the DevOps movement and the Cloud Native ecosystem, distributed systems as well as cybersecurity and DevSecOps. I am also the co-founder and lead organizer of the Cloud Native Dakar Community (CNCF Dakar or CNCG Dakar) and casual speaker. Project Experience: Kubernetes, Argo, Prometheus, Istio, KEDA, Helm

person.name: Mohan Singh Bartwal
person.bio: I am passionate about infrastructure observability and data democratization(KPI/SLI/SLO). Skilled in consolidating and integrating observability data across organizational silos, enabling horizontal and vertical data sharing to empower data-driven decision-making. Led IT infrastructure teams, to drive cloud migration-transformations. Encouraged and mentored team to enhance operational excellence, deliver high availability, business continuity, and disaster recovery solutions. Member of the ISC2 and PMI BayArea local chapters.

person.name: Toluwanimi E. Banji-Idowu
person.bio: Hi, I'm Tolu—a DevSecOps Engineer passionate about building secure, scalable cloud-native architectures. With expertise in CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), and container orchestration, I specialize in implementing security-first practices across multi-cloud environments. I'm actively expanding my knowledge in CNCF technologies, particularly Kubernetes and cloud-native security patterns, and looking forward to contributing to CNCF projects while sharing my journey and technical insights.

person.name: Blake Shaver
person.bio: I am a devops engineer specializing in automation tools in a hybrid environment. I frequently work with Ansible Automation Platform and work to develop pipelines and controls. I am interested in cloud technologies and containers and push myself to learn new tools to implement new solutions. I also hold certifications in Linux, Ansible, AWS, Jenkins and Terraform.

person.name: Toon Van Deuren
person.bio: Focussed on building platforms with Kubernetes, Crossplane and Azure. Combining technical know-how with boundless curiosity and a collaborative knowledge-sharing spirit. I enjoy teaching and mentoring others. I'm currently working as a freelance consultant. You'll find me blogging and talking about cloud-native technologies in my spare time.

person.name: Vinothkumar Kuppusamy
person.bio: I'm a Principal Engineer with extensive experience in designing and delivering scalable Digital Infrastructure Solutions. As a technology enthusiast, I actively explores emerging cloud-native ecosystems and cloud-native best practices to enhance system reliability, security, and performance. I always have a passion for Learning & Mentoring which drives both my professional growth and technical leadership.

person.name: Yared Taye Tolosa
person.bio: I am a Senior Systems Architect with a strong background in Digital Identity,  Decentralized Identity systems and Linux kernel Development. I specialize in cloud-native technologies, with hands-on experience in Kubernetes, container orchestration, and distributed system design. My work focuses on building secure and scalable platforms, particularly for national-scale identity solutions. I am passionate about open-source and continuously explore innovations within the CNCF ecosystem.

person.name: Bijay Pathak
person.bio: I’m a DevOps Engineer and Platform Engineer with a deep-seated passion for Kubernetes and the evolving cloud-native landscape. I specialize in architecting secure, production-grade infrastructure on AWS and enjoy solving complex challenges related to large-scale migrations and system performance. I am driven by the goal of building highly efficient, cost-effective platforms that balance robust security with an exceptional developer experience. Beyond technical delivery, I care deeply about mentoring the next generation of engineers, sharing practical knowledge, and contributing to the growth of the global cloud-native community.

person.name: Dongho Han
person.bio: As a Cloud Architect at Samsung SDS, I design PaaS products like API Gateways. My background includes Java backend development and extensive experience in various SI projects. I've translated "Developing Microservices with Spring Boot" into Korean and served as an in-house technical instructor, sharing knowledge with colleagues.  With my passion for cloud-native technologies and diverse skill set, I'm eager to contribute to various CNCF projects. I look forward to leveraging my experience and expertise to foster innovation and collaboration within the CNCF ecosystem, aiming to make meaningful contributions across multiple areas.

person.name: Jianfeng Zhang
person.bio: I am engaged in cloud computing work and have started learning Kubernetes in the past two years. I hope to gain more knowledge about K8s and meet like-minded friends through CNCF.

person.name: Nguyễn Hữu Hiệp
person.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer. Knowing about Kubernetes is absolutely wonderful for me. Everything is just the beginning, so keep pushing and working hard!

person.name: Jared Price
person.bio: I'm a DevSecOps engineer passionate about Kubernetes, cloud native technologies, and the art of continuous learning. I believe in being a '1.01x engineer' - focusing on small, daily improvements that compound over time. I spend my days building secure infrastructure and automating deployments, while constantly exploring new ways to enhance my craft. When I'm not tinkering with containers or learning something new, you can find me cheering on the Green Bay Packers and Arsenal.

person.name: Julio Cesar Caraca

I actively contribute to the community by organizing meetups and speaking at technical events, sharing practical experiences around containers, automation, and DevOps practices.

person.name: Mücahit Ündemir
person.bio: I am a Devops Consultant at WhizUs and working at the moment on Azure, managing AKS clusters with Terraform, implementing GitOps with ArgoCD, etc.  I have joined a few Meetups in the last months which was organized WhizUs: https://www.meetup.com/de-DE/vienna-devops-security/ At the next Meetup, I want to hold a talk about ArgoCD App-of-Apps and Applicationsets, it will be a start for me to hold talks at Meetups  We have joined also the KCD Austria 2023 and KubeCon Paris this year.

person.name: Radu Dobrinescu
person.bio: Senior Cloud Solutions Architect specializing in cloud-native architectures and Kubernetes orchestration. Focusing on designing scalable solutions using Amazon EKS and implementing AI/ML training and inference on Kubernetes. Actively involved in the AWS Bucharest User Group Meetup and passionate about helping organizations modernize through cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Silvan Loser
person.bio: I’m a Site Reliability Engineer and experienced Linux specialist with a strong focus on Kubernetes in both on-premise and cloud environments. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies, automation, and simplifying complex systems. I enjoy collaborating closely with diverse teams to create efficient, reliable, and scalable solutions — and to push the boundaries of what’s possible in the Kubernetes ecosystem.

person.name: Aakansha Priya
person.bio: Aakansha Priya is DevRel at EmpathyOps, a CNCF Ambassador and an organiser for KubeHuddle, Toronto. Working on empowering & upscaling the communities. In the past, she has been a Community Manager at Hashnode & WeMakeDevs, experienced in working efficiently in groups as well as individually.  As a frontend dev turned DevOps explorer, loves sharing tech insights, and views on learning & building in public, blogging & building personal brand. Aakansha, a reader and speaker, paints, sings, and loves spontaneous travel. She has spoken at various conferences about DevOps and the human side of tech. Catch her at meetups & conferences!

person.name: Kasper Borg Nissen
person.bio: Co-founder of the Nordic meetup alliance, Cloud Native Nordics, where he also organize the Aarhus group, and tries to help the nordic community as much as possible, e.g. setting up KCDs and more. Kasper also serves as Co-Chair for KubeCon+CloudNativeCon EU/NA 2024/2025. He works as a Developer Relations Engineer at Dash0, previously at Lunar where he in the past has shared many experiences with Cloud Native tech at meetups and conferences. Kasper wrote his master thesis, “KubeCloud - A Small Scale Cloud Computing Environment”, on how Universities could leverage small-scale cloud environments built out of Raspberry Pis running Kubernetes.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador, because it's a great community of like-minded people. I hope to meet and learn from the other awesome Ambassadors, and help grow the adoption of Cloud Native technologies even more here in the Nordics.

person.name: Sivagandhan Jeyakkodi
person.bio: I am a passionate Cloud, Linux, and Kubernetes enthusiast with a Master's Degree in Computer Networking and a Bachelor's Degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering. My expertise spans across various domains, and I hold several valuable certifications in Kubernetes, AWS, RedHat, and Cisco.  With over 9 years of hands-on experience in the IT field, I have gained a deep understanding of Private and Public Cloud (AWS), Kubernetes, Networking (Cisco switches, Routers & ASA Firewall), Virtualization (VMWare and Xen Hypervisors), Windows and Linux Administration, as well as Python & Golang Programming.
